data.title: ONWARD Medical appoints Ali Kiboro as CFO
Germany's flash PMI numbers show business activity growth in January
data.description:
• The HCOB Flash Germany Composite PMI Output Index came in at 52.5 in January, up from 51.3 in December and the highest for three months. 
  • Flash Germany Services PMI Business Activity Index at 53.3 vs. December's 52.7, a 3-month high.
  • HCOB Flash Germany Manufacturing PMI at 48.7, compared to 47 last month, a 3-month high.
  • “The data show a good start to the new year, overall. Output in the manufacturing sector returned to - albeit meagre - growth, and so did new orders. Even more encouraging is the stronger pickup in activity in the services sector. Looking ahead, confidence has risen noticeably in the services sector and has held at a solid level in manufacturing. “While there are signs of a modest recovery, services companies have trimmed their workforce significantly in January, which might point more to efficiency measures than to concerns about demand. In manufacturing, the process of cutting jobs has continued unabated. Since this has been ongoing since mid-2023, there’s now a lot of debate about whether this is a structural issue, one that would require structural answers in the form of reforms that are anything but easy to implement." said Dr. Cyrus de la Rubia, Chief Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank.

data.title: France flash composite PMI at three-month low
Earnings Snapshot: Alcoa tops Q4 estimates; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
Sandisk rally inflicts $3B loss on shorts, squeeze risk spikes - report
data.description:
Short interest in Sandisk (SNDK) has risen steadily in recent months even as the stock has rallied sharply, pushing the risk of a short squeeze to “extreme” levels, Bloomberg reported, citing data from S3 Partners.

“Short sellers have moved in lockstep over the last few months, utilizing a reversal strategy by shorting aggressively into the rally,” S3 Partners' research team wrote in a note.

Since early November, short interest has climbed to about 7.5% of the float from roughly 4%, while S3’s short-squeeze risk score has jumped to 82.5. Mark-to-market losses on short positions have reached around $3B, driving the score to what the firm describes as extreme risk, S3 said.

Sandisk (SNDK) shares have surged 112% so far this year, outperforming the S&P 500 Index’s gain of about 1% and ranking the stock among the index’s top performers in 2026.

The rally reflects a shift in the artificial intelligence trade toward storage stocks, along with global flash memory shortages that have allowed the company to raise prices, according to S3.

Earlier this month, SanDisk shares surged after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang highlighted rising demand for memory at the CES conference, describing storage as a “completely unserved market.”

The pioneer in flash memory became a public company in 1995 and was then acquired by Western Digital (WDC) in 2016. Just last year, the company was re-established as a stand-alone company, adding almost 1,200% to its stock since then.

data.title: EquipmentShare.com raises $747M in IPO, prices at 24.5 per share
data.description:
• Construction technology firm specializing in equipment rentals and sales, EquipmentShare.com, priced its IPO within its indicated range, ​raising $747.3M and underscoring strong demand for startups looking ‌to tap public markets.
  • The company sold 30.5M shares with an option to purchase up to an additional ~4.58M shares.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 23, 2026, under the ticker symbol “EQPT.”
  • The offering is expected to close on January 26, 2026.

data.title: International Tower Hill prices $65M public and $40M private stock offering
data.description:
• International Tower Hill Mines (THM) priced an upsized offering of 29.29M shares at a price to the public of $2.22 per share.
  • In connection with the offering, Paulson & Co. Inc. has entered into an agreement to purchase $40M shares in a private placement at the public offering price of the offering, bringing total gross proceeds to roughly $105M. 
  • The net proceeds of the offering and the concurrent private placement to fund the exploration and development of the Livengood Gold Project and general corporate purposes.
  • The underwriters have an option to purchase up to 15% additional shares, potentially raising proceeds to $115M if exercised.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 27, 2026.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Intel tops Q4 estimates but initiates Q1 outlook below forecast
UK retail sales rise 2.5% in December
data.description:
• Retail sales in the UK increased 2.50% in December over the same month in the previous year, above the consensus of 1%.

  • Retail sales in the UK increased 0.40% in December of 2025 over the previous month.

data.title: Columbus McKinnon prices senior notes and loans
data.description:
•  Columbus McKinnon (CMCO) priced its offering of $900M in aggregate principal amount of 7.125% senior secured notes due 2033 in connection with the company's previously announced pending acquisition of Kito Crosby.
  • The aggregate size of the offering has been downsized from $1.225M to $900M.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 30, 2026.
  • Besides, the company also priced a new $1.65B aggregate principal amount senior secured term loan B due in 2033. The loans under the facility will be issued at a price equal to 99.0% of their face value and bear interest at SOFR plus 3.50%.
  • Both net proceeds will be used to fund the Kito Crosby acquisition, including settling its debts, alongside sales of preferred shares to CD&R XII Keystone Holdings, L.P., and a new credit agreement and refinancing existing company debt.
